prompt:
Add 1Password and Zen browser to the NixOS desktop configuration.

## What to do

### 1. Add zen-browser flake input
In flake.nix, add a new input:
  zen-browser.url = "github:zen-browser/desktop";
Do NOT add inputs.nixpkgs.follows for it — the zen-browser flake manages its own nixpkgs pin.
Pass it through to modules via the existing inputs specialArgs pattern (already done via extraSpecialArgs = { inherit inputs; }).

### 2. Add 1Password at the system level
In modules/common/desktop.nix, add:
  programs._1password.enable = true;
  programs._1password-gui = {
    enable = true;
    polkitPolicyOwners = [ config.cosmo.user.default ];
  };
This requires access to config, which is already in the function args.

### 3. Add Zen browser at the home level
In home/desktop.nix:
- The file currently takes { config, pkgs, ... }: — change this to { config, pkgs, inputs, ... }: to access the flake inputs.
- Add zen-browser to home.packages: inputs.zen-browser.packages.x86_64-linux.default
- Update MIME associations: change all three google-chrome.desktop entries to use zen.desktop (that is the .desktop file name Zen uses)
- Remove the chrome-flags.conf xdg.configFile (Zen handles Wayland natively)
- Keep the electron-flags.conf but make it standalone (not referencing chrome-flags):
  xdg.configFile."electron-flags.conf".text = literal two single-quotes
    --ozone-platform=wayland
    --enable-features=WaylandWindowDecorations
  literal two single-quotes;

### 4. Update Hyprland browser keybinding
In home/hyprland.nix, find the line with mainMod B Open browser exec google-chrome-stable and change google-chrome-stable to zen.

### 5. Do NOT remove google-chrome from packages
The user may still want Chrome available — just make Zen the default.

### Important notes
- Run nix flake check or at minimum nix eval to verify the config parses. If that is too slow, at least run nix flake lock --update-input zen-browser to verify the input resolves.
- Format all changed .nix files with nixfmt before committing.
- Do not include Co-Authored-By lines in commits.
- Do not mention AI/Claude in commit messages or PR description.
